title: The power of entanglement in distributed quantum machine learning
authors: Yerim Kim, Kiwmann Hwang, Hyukjoon Kwon, Yosep Kim
abstract: The quantum internet aims to interconnect distant devices and enable large-scale computation through distributed quantum algorithms. One of the key obstacles is communication latency during computation. Even separations of a few hundred kilometers introduce millisecond-scale delays, which exceed the coherence times of many solid-state qubit platforms. In contrast, entanglement can be established beforehand and used as a practical resource to reduce communication complexity between remote nodes. Here we examine the utility of entanglement in distributed quantum machine learning for binary classification tasks. Drawing an analogy with the CHSH game, we show that entanglement improves classification accuracy across all datasets considered. We also find that excessive entanglement may degrade performance by reducing the effective dimension of the parameter space. This highlights the importance of using an appropriate amount and structure of entanglement in data embedding. Our findings bridge nonlocality and machine-learning advantage, providing a pathway toward distributed quantum computation beyond coherence-time constraints.
summary: This paper investigates how entanglement can be used to overcome communication delays in distributed quantum machine learning. It demonstrates that while entanglement boosts classification accuracy, there is an optimal amount to use to avoid reducing the model's parameter space. This research offers a pathway for scalable quantum computing across distant nodes.
main_problem: Addressing the issue of communication latency in distributed quantum computing, which can exceed qubit coherence times, by using entanglement as a resource.
main_result: Entanglement improves classification accuracy in distributed quantum machine learning for binary tasks, though excessive entanglement can degrade performance by reducing the effective parameter space dimension.
method: The study examines distributed quantum machine learning for binary classification, drawing an analogy between entanglement utility and the CHSH game.
model_or_system: A distributed quantum machine learning framework for binary classification tasks involving remote nodes connected via entanglement.
key_observables: Classification accuracy and the effective dimension of the parameter space.
important_parameters: Amount and structure of entanglement in data embedding; communication latency/delay scales.
main_assumptions: Entanglement can be established beforehand to mitigate the effects of latency during computation.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of latency in the quantum internet, proposes entanglement as a solution, analyzes its impact on distributed machine learning accuracy, and discusses the optimization of entanglement structure.
why_interesting: It provides a link between nonlocality (CHSH-like correlations) and machine learning advantage, offering a strategy to perform distributed computations despite coherence-time constraints.
analysis_basis: abstract only; PDF text extraction failed or PDF unavailable
